visibleSummary: When pressure rises, you shut down or go quiet.
receiptQuotes: I tend to shut down whenever someone raises their voice at me
Every time I'm given critical feedback, I freeze up and can't respond
I notice that I always go quiet when I feel overwhelmed in group settings
rationale: All three receipts describe shutting down or going quiet under pressure, directly supporting the summary.

visibleSummary: You often tell yourself you can't do it or get it right.
receiptQuotes: I'm terrible at keeping the commitments I make to myself
I can't stop myself from catastrophizing whenever I make a mistake
I always ruin things right when they start going well for me
rationale: Summary claims pre-action incapability framing, but receipts describe post-event catastrophizing and retrospective failure, not anticipatory self-defeat.
notes: Intentional unfaithful case: summary extrapolates beyond what receipts support.

visibleSummary: When pressure rises, you default to pleasing or appeasing.
receiptQuotes: Whenever I feel overwhelmed, I automatically withdraw from everyone around me
I notice that I always default to people-pleasing when someone seems upset
notes: Parse failure case: simulates malformed judge output.

visibleSummary: You describe returning to the same loop even when you see it happening.
receiptQuotes: I keep finding myself back in the same loop with this issue
Over and over I go through this same cycle, I know the pattern well
notes: Schema invalid case: simulates judge response with missing required fields.

visibleSummary: You describe improvement when you stay steady under pressure.
receiptQuotes: I've been doing better at speaking up for myself this week
I finally managed to set a limit and actually stick with it
rationale: Summary broadly captures the improvement theme; both receipts show steady progress through speaking up and setting limits.
notes: Borderline case: summary is somewhat general but well-evidenced by receipts.

visibleSummary: Pressure often pushes you toward avoidance.
receiptQuotes: I tend to avoid difficult conversations entirely whenever I feel any pressure or discomfort
Every time I have something important to do, I end up procrastinating until I am overwhelmed by the deadline
rationale: Both receipts describe avoidance and procrastination under pressure, directly supporting the summary.

visibleSummary: When pressure rises, you default to pleasing or appeasing.
receiptQuotes: I tend to apologize immediately whenever someone seems even slightly upset with me

visibleSummary: Self-doubt shows up when you assess your own ability.
receiptQuotes: I always second-guess my decisions even after I have thoroughly thought them through
I honestly doubt my ability to follow through when something really matters to me
rationale: Both receipts describe self-doubt and second-guessing at the point of decision or action, directly supporting the summary.

visibleSummary: Self-doubt shows up when you assess your own ability.
receiptQuotes: I am terrible at finishing anything I start when there is real pressure involved
I always mess things up on the very day things were starting to look promising for me
rationale: Summary applies a self-doubt/assessment frame but receipts describe incapability framing and self-sabotage — the incapability branch better fits the evidence than the assessment-doubt branch.

visibleSummary: You describe returning to the same loop even when you see it happening.

Over and over I find myself back at square one despite all the work I have put into changing this
rationale: Both receipts explicitly describe returning to the same loop and cycle despite effort, directly matching the summary.

visibleSummary: Progress appears when you follow through consistently.

rationale: Both receipts describe concrete follow-through and consistency in routines, directly supporting the summary.

visibleSummary: You describe improvement when momentum becomes visible.

I am making progress on building the habits I care about and the momentum is finally there

visibleSummary: Progress appears when you follow through consistently.
receiptQuotes: I actually managed to stay calm during a difficult conversation I had been dreading all week
I was able to step away from the situation before I said something I would have deeply regretted
